human_verification.notes: Reviewed issue #1591 acceptance criteria, the refreshed manifest, constrained research summary, functionality map, scenario suite, evaluation results, deployment notes, commercialization notes, specification-based runtime posture, and refusal boundaries.

source_classes_covered: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to security architecture assessment, trust-boundary design, control-to-risk rationale, segmentation guidance, remediation sequencing, and evidence-backed escalation.

Authority sources cover NIST CSF 2.0, CISA Cross-Sector Cybersecurity Performance Goals, NIST SP 800-160, NIST RMF, NIST zero trust architecture references, CISA ZTMM, NIST SP 800-53, CIS Controls, and OWASP ASVS.

Evaluation scenarios cover all required output shapes, normal in-scope work, partial evidence, low-confidence escalation, boundary refusal, adjacent specialist routing, cross-industry cybersecurity specificity, tenant-specific adaptation, conflicting-source escalation, no unreviewed regressions, and materialization readiness.
The package remains specification-based and does not claim a retained retained implementation without separate promotion evidence.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by issue #1591.
Research output records source authority, workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, failure modes, scenario-family coverage, and non-blocking unresolved ambiguity.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated spec pack for security architecture review and design guidance, not as a built execution runtime.
Runtime may remain specification-based unless a separate trusted-build promotion proves frequent use, implementation safety, tenant isolation, and sustained validation performance.
